title: Typed Semantic Definitions Refactor
subtitle: One-off refactor living doc for making living-doc structure and behavior executable without moving concrete docs out of JSON.
scope: Living Doc Compositor registry, templates, MCP diagnostics, skills, generated docs, and contract tests.
sourceCoverage: Completed on 2026-05-06: typed definitions generate the selected living-doc semantic machinery and selected template/registry artifacts while concrete docs remain JSON. Core convergence-type vocabulary remains registry-owned by repository invariant; template example cards remain seed content, not reusable semantic machinery.
objective: Integrate typed semantic definitions into Living Doc Compositor so living-doc structure and behavior have one executable source that generates the registry/template artifacts, relationship graphs, MCP diagnostics, tests, and reference diagrams while concrete living doc instances remain JSON.
successCondition: The refactor is complete when typed definitions are the maintained executable source for the intended living-doc semantic machinery, generate the selected registry/template artifacts deterministically, preserve existing JSON/HTML rendering and sync flows, drive MCP relationship/stage diagnostics and valid operations, support skill use through concrete patchable operations, and have contract tests that prevent drift. Individual implementation slices are checkpoints toward this full integration, not alternative objectives or completion claims.

issueReferences.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Prove generated semantic patch workflow in a real living-doc run
Decide and implement the full template/registry generation boundary
Expose generated semantic relationships and stages in the compositor UI
Expand typed semantic definitions beyond the first three template families
Finalize typed semantic definitions epic completion proof

sourceReferences.title: Living Doc Stage Skill Operating Model
Living Doc Knowledge Graph, MCP, and Skill Model
Living Doc Code Representation Value Model

objectiveFacets.name: Target architecture
objectiveFacets.description: Typed definitions become the executable source for reusable semantic machinery while concrete living doc instances remain JSON artifacts.

objectiveFacets.name: Migration path
objectiveFacets.description: The refactor lands through reversible implementation steps that begin with one template family and expand only after proof.

objectiveFacets.name: Behavioral proof
objectiveFacets.description: Generated diagnostics must prove they add relationship or stage insight beyond the current hand-coded local section diagnostics.

objectiveFacets.name: Drift control
objectiveFacets.description: Generated artifacts, MCP behavior, rendered docs, and skill context stay aligned through deterministic generation and contract tests.

objectiveFacets.name: Decision trace
objectiveFacets.description: Settled architecture decisions and implementation constraints remain explicit while the refactor moves from additive graph metadata toward broader generated semantic machinery.

invariants.name: Concrete docs stay JSON
invariants.statement: Do not move concrete living doc instance content into code; code may define semantic machinery and generate artifacts, but living docs remain portable JSON artifacts.

invariants.name: Prove before expanding
invariants.statement: Do not migrate broad registry or template authorship until one generated template graph improves MCP diagnostics beyond existing structure reflection.

invariants.name: Generated artifacts stay readable
invariants.statement: Generated JSON and docs must remain committed, readable, deterministic, and compatible with the compositor and GitHub Pages workflow.

invariants.name: Objective is settled
invariants.statement: Do not treat remaining implementation checkpoints as open direction decisions; the objective is full integration of typed semantic definitions as executable semantic machinery while concrete living docs stay JSON.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.rationale: Shows the current stage of the refactor at a glance so the integration objective does not disappear behind detailed architecture, proof, and tooling sections.

callout.title: Completion proof recorded
items: Typed definitions now generate selected template identity, section shape, semantic definitions, graphs, diagrams, registry semantic uses, rendered semantic context, MCP diagnostics, and contract-checked skill surfaces.
Core registry vocabulary remains registry-owned because the repo invariant says the registry is the source of truth for convergence types.
Template example cards remain hand-authored seed content because they are example state, not reusable semantic machinery.

sections.title: Formal Model
sections.rationale: Defines the target semantic model for the refactor: what becomes executable typed machinery, what remains generated artifact, and what remains concrete JSON document state.

data.name: Typed semantic definitions as source of machinery
data.definition: Reusable living-doc semantic machinery is authored as typed definitions for types, templates, expected relationships, stage signals, diagnostics, and operations; generated JSON/metadata remain the artifacts consumed by the compositor, renderer, MCP server, tests, and docs.
data.authorityScope: Registry/template semantics, template relationship graphs, MCP stage diagnostics, valid operations, generated prompt context, and generated diagrams.
notes: This model does not replace concrete living doc JSON instances.
The first definition source should be additive and prove value on one template family.
violations: Moving concrete doc content into code would break the artifact boundary.
Generating artifacts that are not deterministic, readable, or compatible with the compositor would fail the refactor objective.

data.name: Generated template relationship graph
data.definition: A generated graph captures template section order, expected type relationships, stage signals, and valid operations so MCP can reason over relationships rather than only local section shape.
data.authorityScope: Read-only MCP diagnostics and generated documentation for template relationship maps.
notes: The generated graph includes machine-readable stage signal conditions, not only explanatory `when` text.
MCP evaluates those conditions generically for current docs rather than hardcoding template-specific stage logic.
The model now covers four different families: surface delivery, proof/canonicality, operations support, and OSS issue deep-dive.

sections.title: Model Assertions
sections.rationale: Keeps the core claims of the refactor explicit so implementation does not proceed on hidden assumptions about JSON, code representation, MCP value, or proof requirements.

data.name: The full integration objective is settled
notes: The refactor is not exploring whether typed definitions should become executable semantic machinery; that direction is already the objective.

The implementation questions are sequencing and proof questions: which type/template surface to migrate next, which generated artifacts become canonical next, how MCP and skills use the generated behavior, and which contracts prove the system is integrated.
Small implementation slices should be recorded as checkpoints toward this objective, not as separate strategies or endpoints.

data.name: Living doc instances remain JSON artifacts
notes: JSON is still the correct representation for stored living doc state because it is portable, inspectable, patchable, renderable, and archival.

data.name: Code defines behavior over living-doc state
notes: Stage signal behavior now lives in typed definitions as condition objects and is evaluated by MCP generically.
This is the first concrete step where code representation carries behavior, not only documentation or generated metadata.

data.name: MCP diagnostic benefit must be proven before broad migration
notes: The first experiment succeeds only if generated graph diagnostics identify relationship or stage gaps that current section-local diagnostics do not express clearly.

sections.title: Design-Code-Spec Flow
sections.rationale: Tracks the implementation path toward full integration, not a set of optional experiments; each card should show how the refactor moves closer to typed definitions becoming the maintained executable source.

data.name: Full refactor integration path

ticketIds.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Prove generated semantic patch workflow in a real living-doc run
Decide and implement the full template/registry generation boundary
Expose generated semantic relationships and stages in the compositor UI
Expand typed semantic definitions beyond the first three template families
Finalize typed semantic definitions epic completion proof

notes: The full refactor objective is now treated as reached for the selected semantic machinery: typed definitions generate the reusable behavior/structure surfaces and selected registry/template artifacts while concrete docs remain JSON.

Generated surfaces include template identity, section shape, semanticDefinition blocks, semantic graph JSON, diagram JSON, registry semanticUses, MCP diagnostics, rendered semantic context, skill routing contracts, and drift tests.
The two remaining hand-authored surfaces are not hidden gaps: template example cards are seed state, and core registry vocabulary remains registry-owned by repository invariant.
Future expansion should add more typed template families or richer evidence rules, not reopen this objective unless a concrete drift pattern proves the current boundary insufficient.

data.name: First PR: additive surface-delivery template graph
notes: Initial implementation slice landed: typed definitions for surface-delivery, generated template graph artifact, contract test, MCP template graph tool, and semanticGraph metadata in structure reflection.
Still partial because relationship-gap and stage-diagnostic tools are not implemented yet, and only one template family is covered.

data.name: Extend structure reflection with generated semantic graph
notes: `living_doc_structure_reflect` includes generated semantic graph metadata when the doc matches a known template graph.
Dedicated MCP tools now expose relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, and valid operations for generated template graphs.
Still partial because diagnostics are conservative section/card-population checks; they do not yet inspect rich card-level edges.

sections.title: Code Anchors
sections.rationale: Pins the refactor to the concrete files that currently own registry, templates, MCP behavior, tests, and publishing so the migration stays source-grounded.

data.name: Current registry JSON
data.what_it_does: Defines convergence types, entity types, status sets, prompt guidance, source keys, text fields, and rendering metadata.
why_it_matters: This remains the committed artifact consumed by renderer, compositor, MCP, and GitHub Pages.
Typed definitions may eventually generate selected registry fields, but not in the first implementation step.

data.name: Surface delivery template
data.what_it_does: Provides the first candidate template family for generated relationship graph proof.
why_it_matters: The template has a clear expected chain: status -> design/code/spec flow -> alignment -> verification -> tooling.
That chain is currently described in docs and examples but not executable as generated semantic graph metadata.

data.name: Living doc MCP server
data.what_it_does: Exposes registry, objective, structure, source, coverage, governance, patch, and render tools to inference-time agents.
why_it_matters: The MCP surface already has behavior; the refactor should feed it generated relationship/stage metadata rather than inventing a parallel tool surface.

data.name: Compositor embed sync
data.what_it_does: Embeds registry, templates, profiles, renderer, semantic context helper, and helper scripts into the standalone compositor and mirrored docs/scripts assets.
why_it_matters: Generated artifacts must preserve this publishing path; changing source authorship must not break rendered docs or GitHub Pages.

data.name: Typed definition source
data.what_it_does: Defines the surface-delivery semantic graph source: section roles, expected relationships, card-level evidence rules, machine-readable stage signal conditions, and valid operations.
why_it_matters: This is the first executable semantic definition that generates graph metadata instead of hand-maintaining that relationship model only in prose.
The flow-to-alignment relationship now carries a concrete evidence rule for shared card values.

data.name: Generated template graph artifact
data.what_it_does: Stores the generated semantic graph consumed by MCP and contract tests, including relationship evidence rules when definitions provide them.
why_it_matters: Keeps the generated relationship/stage/operation graph inspectable and committed while code remains the source.

data.name: Relationship and stage MCP tools
data.what_it_does: Exposes generated template graph and diagram metadata, compares living docs to expected section and card-level relationships, and returns relationship gaps, repair operations, likely stage candidates, and valid operations.
why_it_matters: This is the first point where typed semantic definitions produce inference-time behavior and inspectable representation rather than only a generated artifact.
Relationship diagnostics now distinguish populated-but-unlinked cards from genuinely connected cards where evidence rules exist.
Relationship gaps now include generated repair operations so skills can move directly from diagnosis to a bounded next action.
Relationship gaps now include minimal `living-doc-ai-patch/v1` drafts for direct card-create repairs where the target section exists.
Patch drafts include validation results from the existing patch validator so a skill can distinguish an immediately applicable draft from one that needs editing.

data.name: Semantic-aware skill instructions
data.range: READ AND ASSESS / UPDATE DURING WORK / Key Principles
data.what_it_does: Requires the bootstrap living-doc skill to inspect generated semantic context, relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, valid stage operations, and validated patch drafts when a document matches a generated template graph.
why_it_matters: This makes the generated semantic machinery part of the operator workflow instead of leaving it as an optional MCP surface.
The skill now treats patch drafts as reviewed operation proposals that must be validated and applied through the patch tools.

data.name: Semantic-aware inference harness instructions
data.range: Tool Assumptions / Select Structure / Reflect On Structure / Tool Sequence
data.what_it_does: Requires complex living-doc runs to use generated semantic context, relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, valid operations, and reviewed patch drafts during structure selection and reflection.
why_it_matters: This connects typed semantic definitions to the run harness that actually performs objective work.
The harness keeps the objective-completion boundary while using generated semantic diagnostics to choose concrete document repair operations.

data.name: Proof-canonicality typed definition source
data.what_it_does: Defines the proof-canonicality semantic graph source: model/assertion/proof/finding/decision/tooling relationships, assertion-to-proof evidence, stage conditions, and valid operations.
why_it_matters: This second family tests whether the typed semantic graph model generalizes beyond product surface delivery.
The assertion-to-proof relationship now checks whether proof rungs identify the claim, primitive, invariant, or authority they support.

data.name: Operations-support typed definition source
data.what_it_does: Defines the operations-support semantic graph source: request flow, operating surface, enablers, tooling relationships, operation-to-surface evidence, stage conditions, and valid operations.
why_it_matters: This third family tests whether the typed semantic graph model covers support and mediated workflow docs, not only delivery and proof docs.
The operation-to-surface relationship now checks whether support lanes identify the operation, workflow path, or request input they support.

data.name: Semantic diagram generator
data.what_it_does: Generates Mermaid relationship diagram source from the generated semantic template graph.
why_it_matters: This is the first generated documentation projection from the typed semantic graph, reducing drift between runtime metadata and explanatory diagrams.

data.name: Template artifact semantic generator
data.what_it_does: Writes generated `templateMeta.semanticDefinition` blocks into the selected template JSON artifacts from the generated graph and diagram outputs.
why_it_matters: This moves the selected template artifacts into the typed-definition integration chain instead of leaving semantic machinery only in sidecar generated JSON.
The compositor, embedded templates, rendered template snapshots, MCP context, and contract tests can now see the same generated semantic definition block on the template artifact itself.

data.name: Registry semantic-use generator
data.what_it_does: Writes generated `semanticUses` blocks into registry convergence types used by the typed template families.
why_it_matters: This makes the registry aware of generated template roles, incoming and outgoing relationships, stage signals, and graph provenance for each selected convergence type.
The compositor and mirrored docs/scripts registry now carry the same generated semantic-use metadata as the source registry.

data.name: Semantic context helper
data.what_it_does: Centralizes semantic graph loading, diagram loading, template inference, semantic graph summaries, rendered semantic context construction, and rendered HTML semantic-context extraction.
why_it_matters: Renderer and MCP now share the same template-inference behavior instead of carrying duplicate docId/canonicalOrigin/section-sequence matching logic.
MCP can now retrieve semantic context from either source JSON or standalone rendered HTML.

data.name: Rendered semantic context embed
data.what_it_does: Embeds generated semantic graph and Mermaid diagram context into standalone rendered HTML when the document matches a known semantic template.
why_it_matters: Standalone snapshots now carry the generated relationship context needed by downstream tools, rather than relying only on repo-local generated JSON files.

data.name: Issue #164 real-run proof living doc
data.what_it_does: Records the real living-doc run where generated semantic MCP diagnostics proposed, validated, and applied a patch that repaired the `flow-feeds-alignment` gap.
why_it_matters: This moves the semantic patch workflow proof out of a fixture-only contract and into an actual rendered living doc artifact tied to GitHub issue #164.

ticketIds.title: Prove generated semantic patch workflow in a real living-doc run

data.name: OSS issue deep-dive typed definition
data.what_it_does: Defines the OSS issue deep-dive semantic template family: sections, relationships, stage signals, valid operations, and repairable symptom-to-code-anchor evidence.
why_it_matters: This closes the coverage-expansion gate by proving the typed definition machinery is not limited to the first three families.
The selected family has a different operational shape from delivery/proof/support templates, so it is a stronger coverage test for MCP relationship and stage diagnostics.

ticketIds.title: Expand typed semantic definitions beyond the first three template families

data.name: Compositor semantic preview tab
data.range: Semantics preview tab and renderSemanticPreview helpers
data.what_it_does: Adds a read-only Semantics preview tab that renders generated template semantic definitions as section roles, relationship gap status, stage signals, valid operations, and MCP patch-draft availability.
why_it_matters: Generated semantic metadata is now inspectable by humans in the compositor, not only through JSON, MCP, and contract tests.
The surface stays read-only so patch application remains an explicit MCP/tooling flow until a separate edit design exists.

ticketIds.title: Expose generated semantic relationships and stages in the compositor UI

data.name: Semantic preview browser coverage
data.range: shows generated template semantics without layout overlap
data.what_it_does: Loads the OSS issue deep-dive template, opens the Semantics tab, checks generated semantic text, and verifies top semantic panels have visible non-overlapping bounding boxes.
why_it_matters: This gives #166 browser coverage for nonblank generated semantic UI and basic layout sanity.

ticketIds.title: Expose generated semantic relationships and stages in the compositor UI

data.name: Generated template identity and section shape
data.what_it_does: Writes typed-definition-owned template title, subtitle, scope, objective, success condition, section titles, section convergence types, section rationales, and semanticDefinition blocks into selected template JSON artifacts.
why_it_matters: This closes the earlier gap where typed definitions generated semantic blocks while template identity and section shape still had a separate hand-authored source.
Contract tests now fail if selected template JSON identity or section shape drifts from the typed definition source.

ticketIds.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Finalize typed semantic definitions epic completion proof

sections.title: Attempt Log
sections.rationale: Records probes and migration attempts as they happen so learning accumulates without prematurely turning every experiment into a decision or reusable template.

data.name: Externalize the operating model into three internal pages
what_tried: Created detailed static pages for stage-aware skills, graph/MCP substrate, and code representation value.
what_proved: The concept can be explained coherently enough to seed a refactor issue and this one-off living doc.
notes: This is a thinking artifact, not proof that the implementation path works.

data.name: Capture refactor as GitHub issue #163
what_tried: Created a phased refactor ticket with first PR scope, MCP additions, generated graph artifact, and validation plan.
what_proved: There is a concrete enough implementation plan to track outside conversation.

data.name: Create one-off refactor living doc before making a template
what_tried: Represented the refactor with existing convergence types rather than adding a reusable refactor template prematurely.
what_proved: Pending: whether this doc shape stays useful during implementation.

data.name: Implement additive typed definition and template graph slice
what_tried: Added typed definition source for surface-delivery, generated graph artifact, contract test, MCP template graph tool, and semantic graph metadata in structure reflection.
what_proved: A typed definition can generate a deterministic relationship/stage/operation graph and expose it through the existing MCP surface without replacing living doc JSON artifacts.
notes: This proves additive integration, not the full relationship-gap diagnostic value yet.

data.name: Expose relationship gaps and stage diagnostics through MCP
what_tried: Added MCP tools for relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, and valid stage operations over generated template graphs.
what_proved: The generated surface-delivery graph can drive inference-time diagnostics beyond raw graph lookup: a synthetic doc now triggers a Coherence-stage gap from flow to alignment.
notes: This is still structural diagnostics, not full semantic edge validation.

data.name: Move stage signal behavior into typed definitions
what_tried: Added condition objects to surface-delivery stage signals and changed MCP stage diagnostics to evaluate condition kinds generically.
what_proved: The typed definition layer can carry executable stage behavior rather than leaving MCP to hardcode template-specific stage logic.
notes: This condition language is deliberately small and should grow only when a second template family needs it.

data.name: Add proof-canonicality as second typed template family
what_tried: Added proof-canonicality typed template definition with relationships, stage signal conditions, and valid operations; regenerated the semantic graph and extended MCP contracts.
what_proved: The generated graph and condition evaluator are not limited to surface delivery; the same mechanism can diagnose assertion/proof gaps in a proof-bearing doc shape.
notes: This still uses structural card population as the evidence of relationship strength; card-level semantic edge validation remains future work.

data.name: Generate template relationship diagrams from semantic graph
what_tried: Added `scripts/generate-living-doc-semantic-diagrams.mjs` and generated `scripts/generated/living-doc-template-diagrams.json` from the semantic graph.
what_proved: The relationship graph can already generate Mermaid diagram source for the covered template families, reducing future hand-maintained diagram drift.
notes: The internal model page references the generated artifact but still keeps richer hand-authored explanatory diagrams for now.

data.name: Expose generated template diagrams through MCP
what_tried: Added `living_doc_template_diagrams` to the living-doc MCP server with direct template lookup and doc-shape inference.
what_proved: Generated relationship diagrams can be retrieved at inference time from the same semantic source as graph diagnostics, without asking a skill to reconstruct the shape from prose.
notes: `tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs` now checks surface-delivery and proof-canonicality diagram lookup.

data.name: Add operations-support as third typed template family
what_tried: Added operations-support typed template definition with request-flow, operating-surface, enabler, and tooling relationships; regenerated graph and diagram artifacts; extended contract coverage.
what_proved: The typed semantic graph model can carry support/workflow documents as well as delivery and proof documents, and the same MCP diagnostics can identify an operation-to-support-surface gap.
notes: This is still hand-authored typed definition coverage, not generated registry or template JSON replacement.

data.name: Embed generated semantic context in rendered snapshots
what_tried: Added `scripts/living-doc-semantic-context.mjs`, used it from both MCP and renderer, and embedded `doc-semantic-context` JSON in rendered HTML for matching template docs.
what_proved: Generated graph and diagram context can travel with standalone HTML snapshots, so semantic template knowledge is no longer available only through repo-local MCP reads.
notes: `tests/contract/render.spec.mjs` now renders a copied surface-delivery template and verifies the embedded semantic graph and diagram source.
This still embeds semantic context as a machine-readable script payload; no visible UI has been added for it yet.

data.name: Expose semantic context retrieval through MCP
what_tried: Added `living_doc_semantic_context` so inference-time tools can retrieve full graph and diagram context from either living doc JSON or rendered HTML snapshots.
what_proved: The embedded snapshot context is not just archival; it can be recovered through MCP and used as the same semantic reasoning substrate as source JSON.
notes: `tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs` now renders a copied surface-delivery template and reads its embedded semantic context back through MCP.

data.name: Add first card-level relationship evidence rule
what_tried: Added a `shared-field-value` evidence rule to the surface-delivery flow-to-alignment relationship and taught `living_doc_relationship_gaps` to report missing card evidence when both sections are populated but unlinked.
what_proved: Generated relationship diagnostics can now catch a real semantic gap beyond empty sections: an alignment card can exist while still not evidencing the surface-flow card it claims to judge.
notes: `tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs` now includes a surface-delivery document where flow and alignment are both populated but the alignment row points at the wrong Figma/code target.
This is intentionally one evidence kind before expanding the relationship language.

data.name: Extend card-level evidence rules across template families
what_tried: Added `shared-field-value` evidence rules to proof-canonicality assertion-to-proof and operations-support operation-to-surface relationships.
what_proved: The first card-level evidence mechanism is no longer surface-delivery-specific; the same generated rule shape can detect unlinked proof rungs and unlinked support lanes.
notes: `tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs` now includes populated-but-unlinked evidence-gap documents for surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, and operations-support.

data.name: Attach repair operations to relationship gaps
what_tried: Added `repairOperationIds` to typed relationships and returned matching valid operation objects on MCP relationship gaps.
what_proved: Generated diagnostics can now be actionable: the same relationship definition that detects an evidence gap can tell a skill which operation should repair it.
notes: Surface-delivery suggests `add-alignment-row`; proof-canonicality suggests `add-proof-rung` or `weaken-assertion`; operations-support suggests `add-operating-surface-card`.

data.name: Add patch drafts to relationship gaps
what_tried: Added conservative `living-doc-ai-patch/v1` drafts to MCP relationship gaps when a card-create repair operation and target section are available.
what_proved: Generated diagnostics can now carry a concrete editable operation, not just a question and suggested operation label.
notes: The draft uses the relationship evidence rule to choose the target link field, such as `sourceCardIds`, `assertionIds`, or `operationIds`.
The draft is intentionally not auto-applied; it is a structured starting point for a skill or user to review.
The MCP response now includes the draft validation result from the same validator exposed by `living_doc_patch_validate`.
`tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs` now applies a generated surface-delivery patch draft through `living_doc_patch_apply` and verifies the original relationship gap is resolved.

data.name: Generate semantic blocks into selected template artifacts
what_tried: Added a template artifact generator that writes generated `templateMeta.semanticDefinition` blocks into the surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, and operations-support template JSON files.
Re-rendered the three template HTML snapshots so standalone template pages carry the generated semantic definition through the embedded document payload.
what_proved: The selected template JSON artifacts can now participate in the typed-definition generation chain without moving concrete living doc instances out of JSON.
notes: `tests/contract/template-artifacts.spec.mjs` checks that each selected template artifact contains a semantic-definition block matching the generated graph and diagram artifacts.
`npm run test:contract` now includes the template artifact contract.

data.name: Route skills through generated semantic diagnostics
what_tried: Updated the bootstrap living-doc skill and complex inference harness skill to inspect generated semantic context, relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, valid operations, and reviewed patch drafts.
Added a contract test that prevents those skills from dropping the semantic tool path.
what_proved: The generated semantic MCP surface is now part of the instructed skill workflow rather than a sidecar tool an agent may ignore.
notes: Patch drafts remain proposals: the skills require review, validation, and application through `living_doc_patch_validate` and `living_doc_patch_apply`.
`tests/contract/skills-semantic-tools.spec.mjs` checks both skill files for the required semantic and patch tool references.

data.name: Apply a generated relationship patch draft
what_tried: Extended the MCP contract so a generated `flow-feeds-alignment` patch draft is applied to a surface-delivery living doc fixture through `living_doc_patch_apply`.
Re-ran relationship gaps and stage diagnostics after application.
what_proved: The generated semantic path can move from diagnosis to valid operation to patch draft to applied JSON update, and the original relationship gap disappears after application.
notes: The applied draft creates an alignment card linked with `sourceCardIds: ["primary-surface"]`.
After application, `flow-feeds-alignment` no longer reports `missing-target-cards` or `missing-card-evidence`, and stage diagnostics move from Coherence toward the next Operation gap.

data.name: Generate semantic uses into selected registry types
what_tried: Added a registry semantic-use generator that annotates convergence types used by typed template families with generated template roles, relationships, stage signals, and graph provenance.
Synced the generated registry metadata into `docs/scripts/living-doc-registry.json` and the embedded compositor registry.
what_proved: The selected registry artifact can now participate in the typed-definition generation chain, not only the template JSON artifacts and MCP sidecar graph.
notes: `tests/contract/registry-semantic-uses.spec.mjs` checks that generated registry semantic-use blocks match the template graph.
The generator currently annotates 13 convergence types used by surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, and operations-support.

data.name: Split remaining refactor work into epic subtickets
what_tried: Converted #163 into the parent epic and created five subtickets for the remaining completion items.
what_proved: The remaining work is now tracked as concrete completion gates rather than vague open decisions.

ticketIds.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Prove generated semantic patch workflow in a real living-doc run
Decide and implement the full template/registry generation boundary
Expose generated semantic relationships and stages in the compositor UI
Expand typed semantic definitions beyond the first three template families
Finalize typed semantic definitions epic completion proof

notes: #164 satisfied the real-run semantic patch workflow proof.
#167 satisfied typed coverage beyond the first three template families with `oss-issue-deep-dive`.
#165 satisfied the template/registry generation boundary decision.
#166 satisfied UI inspection for generated semantics with the read-only Semantics preview tab.
#168 records final completed/deferred/residual-risk proof for closing the epic.

data.name: Prove semantic patch workflow on a real living doc
what_tried: Created a real repo living doc whose section sequence lets MCP infer the generated `surface-delivery` template.

Ran `living_doc_semantic_context`, `living_doc_relationship_gaps`, `living_doc_stage_diagnostics`, `living_doc_valid_stage_operations`, `living_doc_patch_validate`, `living_doc_patch_apply`, and `living_doc_render` against that document.
Rendered the updated document after the generated patch created the missing alignment carrier card.
what_proved: The original `flow-feeds-alignment` relationship gap disappeared after the generated `add-alignment-row` patch draft was applied to JSON.
Stage diagnostics advanced from Coherence to Judgment after application, showing that the generated relationship graph changed inference-time assessment rather than only editing content.
The same path can operate on a real living doc artifact, not only the contract-test fixture.

ticketIds.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Prove generated semantic patch workflow in a real living-doc run

notes: Before application, MCP reported `flow-feeds-alignment` as `missing-target-cards` and offered `add-alignment-row` as the repair operation.
The applied patch created `semantic-patch-workflow-feeds` with `sourceCardIds: ["semantic-patch-workflow"]`.
After application, `flow-feeds-alignment` was no longer present in relationship gaps; one later-stage gap remained, which is acceptable because #164 only targets removal of the original gap.

data.name: Expand typed coverage to OSS issue deep-dive
what_tried: Added a typed semantic definition for `oss-issue-deep-dive` with six sections, five relationships, five stage signals, and five valid operations.
Generated graph, Mermaid diagram, template semantic-definition block, registry semantic-use blocks, embedded compositor data, and rendered template HTML.
Extended contracts so graph, diagram, and MCP diagnostics cover the new template family.
what_proved: The typed-definition pipeline now covers four template families instead of three.

MCP can infer `oss-issue-deep-dive`, diagnose missing `symptom-localized-by-anchor` relationships, produce a valid `add-code-anchor` patch draft, apply it, and advance stage diagnostics to the next operational gap.
Generated registry semantic uses now annotate 18 convergence types used by typed template families.

ticketIds.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Expand typed semantic definitions beyond the first three template families

notes: `oss-issue-deep-dive` was selected before monitoring/competitor templates because its internal relationships are concrete and patchable inside one issue handoff: symptom -> anchor -> attempt -> finding, with issue orbit and maintainer stance as constraints.
`npm run test:contract` passes with four typed templates after the expansion.

data.name: Settle template and registry generation boundary
what_tried: Reviewed the current generated artifact surface: semantic graph, Mermaid diagrams, template `semanticDefinition` blocks, registry `semanticUses`, MCP diagnostics, rendered semantic context, and contracts.
Compared that surface with the #165 options: full template JSON generation, selected metadata generation, registry core-field generation, and contract-only semantic blocks.
Recorded the chosen boundary as a decision rather than adding a new generator prematurely.
what_proved: The current useful generation boundary is semantic machinery and semantic projections, not full prose-bearing template or registry artifacts.
The existing generators already implement the chosen boundary: generated graph/diagram artifacts, generated template semantic blocks, generated registry semantic-use blocks, and contracts.
Full template JSON and core registry field generation have explicit deferral rationale and future triggers, satisfying #165 without creating a parallel authoring system.

ticketIds.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Decide and implement the full template/registry generation boundary

notes: This is a boundary decision, not a retreat from full integration. Typed definitions remain the executable source for semantic machinery.

The deferral prevents generated code from overwriting template prose, rationale, example data, registry icons, source fields, status fields, and prompt guidance before those surfaces have proven drift pressure.

sections.title: Proof Ladder
sections.rationale: Defines the safety and value proof needed to finish the full integration, so passing a narrow slice is treated as evidence toward completion rather than completion itself.

data.name: Completion proof spans the whole integration path

ticketIds.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Prove generated semantic patch workflow in a real living-doc run
Decide and implement the full template/registry generation boundary
Expose generated semantic relationships and stages in the compositor UI
Expand typed semantic definitions beyond the first three template families
Finalize typed semantic definitions epic completion proof

notes: Correction: this proof is not complete for the full objective.
What is proven: selected semantic machinery works across definitions, generated artifacts, MCP, skills, rendered context, UI inspection, and tests.

What is not yet proven: that the full refactor has integrated typed definitions as the maintained executable source for the complete intended living-doc semantic machinery and all selected registry/template artifacts without source-of-truth ambiguity.
The next proof must explicitly decide whether each remaining source-of-truth surface is completed, deferred outside this objective, or still blocking.

data.name: Selected template artifacts carry generated semantics
notes: `node scripts/generate-living-doc-template-artifacts.mjs --check` verifies generated semantic-definition blocks on all typed template families.

The selected template JSON artifacts now contain `templateMeta.semanticDefinition` with sections, relationships, stage signals, valid operations, counts, generated graph metadata, and generated Mermaid diagram source.
Four selected template HTML snapshots now carry generated semantic context after render, including `oss-issue-deep-dive`.

data.name: Selected registry types carry generated semantic uses
notes: `node scripts/generate-living-doc-registry-semantics.mjs --check` verifies generated semantic-use blocks on all convergence types used by typed template families.
The selected registry entries now contain `semanticUses` with template roles, incoming and outgoing relationships, related stage signals, and graph artifact provenance.
`node scripts/sync-compositor-embeds.mjs` mirrors those generated registry semantics into the standalone compositor and docs/scripts registry.
The generator now annotates 18 convergence types used by surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, operations-support, and oss-issue-deep-dive.

data.name: Generated patch draft repairs a relationship gap
notes: `tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs` now proves `living_doc_relationship_gaps -> patchDraft -> living_doc_patch_validate -> living_doc_patch_apply -> living_doc_relationship_gaps` on a generated surface-delivery doc fixture.
The proof confirms that applying the generated draft changes the JSON document and resolves the original relationship gap.
The previous weaker part, exercising the same path in a non-fixture ongoing living-doc session, is now covered by the #164 proof document.

data.name: Generated template graph is deterministic
notes: `node scripts/generate-living-doc-semantic-graph.mjs --check` is covered by `tests/contract/template-graphs.spec.mjs`.

data.name: Generated graph finds a relationship gap current reflection does not name
notes: `living_doc_relationship_gaps` detects missing or weak expected relationships from generated template graph metadata.
`living_doc_stage_diagnostics` derives stage candidates from machine-readable condition objects in the generated graph.

Contracts now prove section-level gaps for surface-delivery (`flow-feeds-alignment`), proof-canonicality (`assertion-requires-proof`), operations-support (`operation-routes-surface`), and oss-issue-deep-dive (`symptom-localized-by-anchor`).
Contracts now also prove card-level evidence gaps for the first three families and patch application for the OSS issue symptom-to-anchor repair.
Relationship gaps include repair operations generated from the typed relationship definition and patch drafts with validation results.

data.name: Existing contract and render tests remain green
notes: Targeted MCP contract passes with generated graph lookup, generated diagram lookup, relationship gap diagnostics, stage diagnostics, valid stage operations, patch validation, and patch application across four template families.
Targeted render contract proves matching rendered docs embed `doc-semantic-context` with generated graph and diagram payloads.
`npm run test:contract` passes after expanding typed semantic coverage to `oss-issue-deep-dive`.
`node scripts/sync-compositor-embeds.mjs --check` reports the embedded compositor, registry overview, and docs/scripts mirror are up to date.

data.name: Generated semantic context travels with rendered snapshots
notes: Rendered HTML for matching template docs now includes `<script type="application/json" id="doc-semantic-context">`.
The payload includes template inference method, generated graph template metadata, and generated Mermaid diagram source.
This keeps standalone snapshots compatible with tools that inspect HTML artifacts rather than source JSON in the repo.
`living_doc_semantic_context` can retrieve this payload from rendered HTML snapshots.

data.name: Real-run semantic patch workflow repairs a living doc

ticketIds.title: Prove generated semantic patch workflow in a real living-doc run

notes: `docs/issue-164-semantic-patch-run.json` was updated through the generated semantic path: context inference, relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, valid operations, patch validation, patch application, and render.
Before application, the document was inferred as `surface-delivery`, stage diagnostics reported Coherence, and `flow-feeds-alignment` was `missing-target-cards`.
`living_doc_patch_apply` applied the generated `card-create` change `repair-flow-feeds-alignment-semantic-patch-workflow` to create `semantic-patch-workflow-feeds` in the alignment section.
After application, the original `flow-feeds-alignment` gap was gone, stage diagnostics advanced to Judgment, and `docs/issue-164-semantic-patch-run.html` was refreshed.

data.name: OSS issue deep-dive typed coverage is generated and diagnostic

ticketIds.title: Expand typed semantic definitions beyond the first three template families

notes: `scripts/living-doc-definitions/templates/oss-issue-deep-dive.mjs` defines the next typed family selected for #167.

Generated artifacts now include `oss-issue-deep-dive` in `scripts/generated/living-doc-template-graphs.json`, `scripts/generated/living-doc-template-diagrams.json`, `docs/living-doc-template-oss-issue-deep-dive.json`, registry `semanticUses`, embedded compositor data, and rendered template HTML.

`tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs` proves MCP graph/diagram lookup, missing `symptom-localized-by-anchor` gap detection, valid `add-code-anchor` patch draft validation/application, gap repair, stage advancement to Operation, and valid stage operations for the new family.
This satisfies #167 acceptance because one additional family is covered by graph, diagram, template semantic block, registry semantic-use block, MCP diagnostics, and contracts.

data.name: Generation boundary is explicit and contract-backed

ticketIds.title: Decide and implement the full template/registry generation boundary

notes: Chosen generated surface: typed definitions generate semantic graph JSON, Mermaid diagram JSON, template `templateMeta.semanticDefinition` blocks, registry `semanticUses` blocks, MCP diagnostics, valid operations, repair operation metadata, rendered semantic context, and contracts.

Hand-authored surface retained: full template JSON structure, template prose fields, template example data, section rationales, registry `sources`, `statusFields`, `textFields`, `detailsFields`, icons, projections, status sets, and `promptGuidance`.

Deferral rationale: those retained surfaces still carry product/editorial judgment and local registry vocabulary; generating them now would increase blast radius before #166 proves the generated semantics are inspectable enough for humans.

Future trigger: revisit full template or core registry generation only when #166 or #168 identifies repeated drift that semantic blocks and contracts cannot prevent, or when adding multiple new typed families requires the same hand-maintained registry/template field edits repeatedly.

data.name: Generated semantics are inspectable in the compositor

ticketIds.title: Expose generated semantic relationships and stages in the compositor UI

notes: `docs/living-doc-compositor.html` now includes a Semantics preview tab for documents with generated `templateMeta.semanticDefinition`.
The tab computes relationship status and stage candidates from current section/card data, and labels MCP patch-draft availability without applying changes.

`tests/e2e/compositor.spec.mjs` covers loading `living-doc-template-oss-issue-deep-dive.json`, opening the Semantics tab, seeing generated relationship text, seeing MCP patch draft availability, and verifying visible non-overlapping top panels.
Standalone rendering remains intact because `node scripts/render-living-doc.mjs` re-rendered the OSS template and refactor living doc after syncing the compositor embed.

data.name: Final epic completion proof

ticketIds.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Finalize typed semantic definitions epic completion proof

completed: Typed semantic definitions exist for surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, operations-support, and oss-issue-deep-dive.
Selected template identity fields and section shape are generated from typed definitions into template JSON artifacts.
Generated graph and Mermaid diagram artifacts are deterministic and contract-checked.
Selected template JSON artifacts carry generated `templateMeta.semanticDefinition` blocks and rendered HTML snapshots carry semantic context.
Registry convergence types used by typed families carry generated `semanticUses` blocks.
MCP exposes generated semantic context, relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, valid operations, repair operations, patch draft validation, patch application, and render support.
Core living-doc skills are instructed and contract-checked to use generated semantic diagnostics and patch validation/application instead of reconstructing the structure from prose.
A real living doc run applied a generated semantic patch draft and removed the original relationship gap.
The compositor exposes generated semantics in a read-only Semantics preview tab with focused browser coverage.
Governance coverage is fingerprinted and fresh for the final proof state.
deferred: More template families can be typed later as expansion work.
Richer content-semantic evidence rules can be added later when concrete domains need them.
Editable UI application of generated patch drafts remains a future UX feature; MCP/tooling remains the apply path.
residualRisks: Relationship evidence is structural and field-value based; it does not yet prove rich semantic correctness of arbitrary card prose.
Only four template families are typed, so future families still need explicit semantic definitions before they get this machinery.

If repeated registry-vocabulary drift appears, a new objective should address registry-code generation directly; it is not part of this completed template-semantic refactor because the registry currently owns convergence vocabulary.
notes: This replaces the earlier premature completion claim with a proof tied to actual generated surfaces and the repository invariant about registry ownership.
The closure boundary is not semantic-block-only anymore: selected template identity and section shape are generated too.
The remaining non-generated fields are either example state or registry-owned vocabulary, not unimplemented typed-template semantic machinery.

notes: Template identity and section shape are generated from typed definitions.
Template example data remains hand-authored because it is concrete seed state, not reusable semantic machinery.
Core registry vocabulary remains hand-authored because the registry is the source of truth for convergence types; typed definitions generate semanticUses into that registry artifact.
No known source-of-truth gap remains for the selected typed semantic machinery.

sections.title: Investigation Findings
sections.rationale: Separates observed facts about the current system from implementation claims and decisions, especially where the existing MCP surface already solves part of the problem.

data.name: The MCP surface is already richer than raw JSON reads
notes: `scripts/living-doc-mcp-server.mjs` already exposes registry, structure, sources, coverage, governance, patch, and render tools.
The refactor should improve the existing MCP behavior with generated relationship/stage metadata rather than create a replacement surface.

data.name: The current gap is relational and stage awareness
notes: Current reflection checks sections locally and governance broadly, but template relationship chains and stage inference are not yet first-class generated machinery.

sections.title: Decision Record
sections.rationale: Preserves settled architecture constraints for the refactor. This section should not imply that the core objective is still open; it should distinguish fixed direction from implementation checkpoints.

data.name: The objective is full typed semantic integration
notes: The direction is settled: typed semantic definitions should become the executable source for living-doc semantic structure and behavior.
Remaining work should be described as implementation checkpoints and proof gates, not as open strategic choices.
A small slice is useful only when it advances the full integration path.

data.name: Do not move living doc instances out of JSON
notes: Typed code definitions are for semantic machinery; concrete living docs remain JSON artifacts.

data.name: Start with additive generated graph metadata
notes: The first PR should not rewrite the registry or existing template JSON.

data.name: Use surface-delivery as the first proof family unless implementation reveals a better fit
notes: Surface delivery has a clear relationship chain and should expose whether generated graph diagnostics add practical value.

data.name: Track remaining work as epic subtickets

ticketIds.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Prove generated semantic patch workflow in a real living-doc run
Decide and implement the full template/registry generation boundary
Expose generated semantic relationships and stages in the compositor UI
Expand typed semantic definitions beyond the first three template families
Finalize typed semantic definitions epic completion proof

notes: #163 and #168 are reopened because the previous closure overstated completion.
Future work must not close the epic by narrowing the objective after the fact. If scope is deferred, the document must explain why it is outside the objective and create follow-up tickets for it.

data.name: Generate template identity and section shape plus semantic blocks

ticketIds.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Decide and implement the full template/registry generation boundary
Finalize typed semantic definitions epic completion proof

notes: Canonical generated surface now includes semantic graph JSON, Mermaid diagram JSON, template title/subtitle/scope/objective/success condition, section titles, section convergence types, section rationales, template `templateMeta.semanticDefinition`, registry `semanticUses`, MCP relationship/stage/operation diagnostics, rendered semantic context, and contracts.
Template example cards remain hand-authored because they are seed state for examples, not reusable semantic machinery.

Core registry definitions remain hand-authored because the registry is the source of truth for convergence types; typed template definitions generate uses of that vocabulary, not the vocabulary itself.
This resolves the previous semantic-block-only gap without creating a parallel registry system.

data.name: Registry remains the convergence-type source of truth

ticketIds.title: Epic: Typed semantic definitions for living-doc structure and stage diagnostics
Finalize typed semantic definitions epic completion proof

notes: Typed template definitions reference convergence types and generate per-template semantic uses into the registry artifact.
They do not generate core convergence-type vocabulary fields such as names, icons, projections, sources, status fields, text/details fields, prompt guidance, AI profiles, or status sets.
That is not an unresolved objective gap: repository instructions explicitly make `scripts/living-doc-registry.json` the source of truth for convergence types.
The refactor objective is satisfied by generating the selected registry artifact surface that belongs to typed template semantics: `semanticUses`.

sections.title: Tooling Surface
sections.rationale: Tracks the generator, contract tests, MCP commands, and render/sync tools that must exist or remain stable for the refactor to be operational.

data.name: Semantic graph generator
data.purpose: Generate template relationship graph metadata from typed semantic definitions.
data.whenToUse: Run after changing typed definitions, and in contract tests to verify generated artifact determinism.
caveats: Covers surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, operations-support, and oss-issue-deep-dive so far.

Generates graph and diagram metadata; `scripts/generate-living-doc-template-artifacts.mjs` writes selected semantic-definition blocks into the matching template JSON artifacts, and `scripts/generate-living-doc-registry-semantics.mjs` writes semantic-use blocks into the registry types used by those templates.
Relationship evidence currently supports the `shared-field-value` rule shape across the typed families.
Relationships can name `repairOperationIds` that resolve to valid operation definitions in MCP gap output.
Relationship gaps can produce minimal `living-doc-ai-patch/v1` draft repairs from the same evidence rule and operation metadata.
Stage conditions are intentionally small and structural: section-empty, related-relationship-gap, source-populated-target-empty, all-populated-no-high-gaps, and manual-review.

data.name: Template graph contract test
data.purpose: Ensure generated graph, diagram, selected template artifact metadata, selected registry semantic-use metadata, and semantic-aware skill instructions are deterministic, reference valid registry/template types and relationships, are exposed through MCP, travel with rendered matching docs, and can be retrieved from rendered snapshots across the typed template families.
data.whenToUse: Run as part of `npm run test:contract` after the generator exists.
data.invocation: node tests/contract/template-graphs.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/template-diagrams.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/template-artifacts.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/registry-semantic-uses.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/skills-semantic-tools.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/render.spec.mjs
caveats: Validates deterministic generation and registry/template references for surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, operations-support, and oss-issue-deep-dive.
Validates generated semantic-definition blocks embedded into the selected template JSON artifacts.
Validates generated semantic-use blocks embedded into the selected registry convergence types.
Validates the core skills reference generated semantic context, relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, valid operations, patch draft review, patch validation, and patch application.
Validates relationship evidence rule shape in the generated graph.
Validates relationship repair operation references against each template's valid operations.
Validates rendered semantic context embedding on surface-delivery.
Validates MCP semantic context retrieval from both source JSON and rendered HTML.

Validates MCP relationship gap detection, repair operation output, patch draft shape, embedded patch validation, patch draft validation, patch draft application, and post-apply gap resolution for generated relationship gaps.
Validates the compositor Semantics tab with Playwright for nonblank generated semantic output and basic top-panel layout sanity.
Will need stronger assertions as generated diagnostics move from section/card population toward richer card-level semantic edges.

data.name: Existing living-doc MCP server
data.purpose: Expose living-doc registry, structure, source, coverage, governance, patch, and render operations to inference-time agents.
data.whenToUse: Use as the integration point for generated relationship/stage metadata.
caveats: Now exposes `living_doc_template_graph`, `living_doc_template_diagrams`, `living_doc_semantic_context`, `living_doc_relationship_gaps`, `living_doc_stage_diagnostics`, and `living_doc_valid_stage_operations`.
`living_doc_structure_reflect` includes semantic graph metadata for matching docs.
Relationship diagnostics validate shared-field card evidence where definitions provide it; broader edge semantics are still intentionally unsupported until needed.
